column1: # doctrine_check.sh manifest — parity-locked CANONICAL:<TAG> blocks that must
# stay byte-identical across every carrier file listed for that tag.
# TAG is the comparison-group key (also the literal in-file
# CANONICAL:<TAG>:BEGIN/END marker, UNLESS the 4th column overrides it — see
# below). The 3rd column, when present, is a sed(1) script applied to that
# carrier's extracted CANONICAL block before hashing — use it to normalize an
# intentional per-carrier tail (e.g. a leaf skill's trailing peer-messaging
# clause) that would otherwise break byte-parity across an otherwise-shared
# family. If the strip transform reduces a carrier's block to an empty
# string, doctrine_check.sh REJECTS that carrier outright (nonzero exit,
# explicit error) rather than letting two empty strings match vacuously. The
# 4th column, when present, is the literal CANONICAL marker to search for in
# that carrier's file when it differs from TAG — needed when one physical
# variant families: give each family its own TAG (e.g. `BANNER-LEAF`) so they
# hash-compare independently, and set the 4th column to the real in-file
# marker (`BANNER`) so extraction still finds the block.
# Append new tags/carriers as new lines; comments (#) and blank lines are
# ignored. A tag needs at least 2 carrier lines to be checkable.
# evolve-config's config-blast-radius sentence sits OUTSIDE the CANONICAL block
# (a separate <!-- CONFIG-ONLY --> line after :END), so the block itself is
# byte-identical across all three carriers and IS registered below.
# evolve-* orchestrator family — CANONICAL blocks shared verbatim across the
# team-spawning evolve skills (mechanized separately by symmetry_check.py; also
# registered here so doctrine_check.sh enforces them in one place).
# CRASH-STALL-CORE — detection paragraph + nudge + re-spawn bullets preceding
# divergent (different resume-context/second-failure semantics) — not a carrier.
# PHANTOM-PATH-GUARD — evolve-* location caveat paragraph, byte-identical
# across all 5 evolve-* skills (no strip-transform, no per-file tail; marker
# BANNER-LEAF — leaf-family BANNER bodies share an identical prefix through
# "...or form/manage a team." and diverge only in an intentional per-skill
# strip transform truncates each body at that shared boundary before hashing
# so parity is asserted on the shared prefix, never the whitelisted tail. The
# 4th column ("BANNER") overrides the search marker since the in-file marker
